summary: Calculation stress test across all Adviser-mode calculators. 86/86 pure JS engine assertions pass + 29/29 retirementEngine regression. UI smoke-tested 7 calculator routes — no NaN/Infinity/undefined leakage. Loan calc backend output matches manual amortisation formula exactly ($3,160.34/mo, $637,722 total interest for $500k @ 6.5% 30y). The two duplicated TAX_BRACKETS/calculateTax implementations (StrategicPlanning.jsx + TrustDistributionAnalysis.jsx) produce identical tax to within $1 across 12 income points — drift is a single-cent rounding artefact only, not a HIGH bug.

issues: Console warnings: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' — ResponsiveContainer parent has 0 height during initial mount. Cosmetic, charts render once layout settles, but should add minHeight to avoid recharts warnings.

action_items: OPTIONAL refactor: extract the duplicated TAX_BRACKETS + calculateTax (StrategicPlanning.jsx L109/117 and TrustDistributionAnalysis.jsx L52/63) into /app/frontend/src/lib/auTax.js. Both already produce identical tax (max $1 rounding diff) but DRY violation is a maintenance risk if FY26-27 brackets change.
OPTIONAL: align CGT_DISCOUNT.smsf in RetirementPlanner.jsx (0.333) with CGT.jsx (1/3 ≈ 0.3333). Drift = $0.0033 per dollar of gain, immaterial but flagged for code-quality.
OPTIONAL: add minHeight to ResponsiveContainer wrappers on calculator pages to silence recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on initial mount.

Note: RetirementReadinessEngine.computeReadiness() is non-deterministic (uses Math.random in projectRetirement). Observed noise is 2-5 score points across 5 runs with 300 sims — within reasonable tolerance, but if exact reproducibility is required, plumb a seedable RNG (e.g. seedrandom) into projectRetirement.
critical_code_review_comments: TAX_BRACKETS duplicated verbatim in two pages — DRY violation. Currently identical (FY24-25 Stage-3) so no functional drift, but extract into shared module before next FY change.

CGT entity-type vocabulary inconsistent: RetirementPlanner uses {personal, joint, trust, company, smsf}; CGT.jsx uses {individual, trust, super, smsf, company}. Standardise to a single enum to prevent future bugs.
computeReadiness uses Monte Carlo via Math.random — same input → ±2-5 point variation between runs. Acceptable for UX, but seedable RNG would make outputs reproducible and testable.

SuperannuationGuarantee.jsx uses SG_RATE = 12% — correct for FY25-26 (current as of Jan 2026, since FY25-26 runs 1 Jul 2025 – 30 Jun 2026). The brief mentioned 11.5% (FY24-25) but the code is correctly forward-rolled.
/app/frontend/src/pages/RetirementPlanner.jsx is 1818 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline. Split into RetirementPlanner + sub-components.
updated_files: /app/frontend/test-loader.mjs (NEW, resolves @/ alias for node test runner)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (29/29 retirement + 86/86 calculation assertions + 7/7 calculator pages render clean, 0 NaN/Infinity)

test_credentials: no auth required; localStorage seeded: wealth_command_compliance_v5='permanent', app_mode='adviser', selected_client=thompson_family
context_for_next_testing_agent: To re-run all calculation tests: `cd /app/frontend && node --experimental-loader ./test-loader.mjs src/lib/calculations.test.mjs` (covers tax bracket duplication, CGT, loan amortisation, SG/SMSF caps, Monte Carlo invariants, readiness engine factors/classify/whatMovesTheNeedle/riskPanel, rules engine R3, scenario ordering). The retirementEngine.test.mjs is the existing 29-test regression — keep both green. The custom test-loader.mjs maps @/ → src/ so node can resolve Vite aliases. UI smoke confirmed loan, cgt, sg-calculator, retirement-confidence, scenario-modelling, retirement-workshop, smsf-optimizer all render without NaN/Infinity. No critical bugs found in this iteration.

calculator_matrix.tax_brackets_dup: PASS — SP and TD return identical tax within $1 across [0,18200,45000,100000,135000,190000,250000,500000]
calculator_matrix.loan_500k_6.5_30y: PASS — calc=$3,160.34/mo formula; UI backend returned $3,160 + $637,722 interest
calculator_matrix.monte_carlo_invariants: PASS — no NaN, P10≤P50≤P90 every year, success% ∈ [0,100]
calculator_matrix.readiness_determinism: PASS within ±5 points (Monte Carlo noise — non-seeded RNG)
calculator_matrix.what_moves_needle: PASS — 3 actions sorted by uplift desc
calculator_matrix.ui_smoke_no_NaN: PASS — /loan-calculator, /cgt, /sg-calculator, /retirement-confidence, /scenario-modelling, /retirement-workshop, /smsf-optimizer all render with no NaN/Infinity/undefined
